hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_desc: Tell your career story in the simple past: was / were and regular verbs + -ed (studied, graduated, worked). Use time markers: yesterday, last year, in 2015 -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 12 -- My Past, My Path
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 12 -- My Past, My Path

listenings.text: Hello Graziele. Let me tell you about my path. I studied medicine in Boston. I graduated in 1998. Then I worked in a small clinic for five years. In 2008, I joined a big hospital. Two years ago, I became the head doctor. It was a long journey, but I love my work today.

Hi Graziele, this is Ana. A short tip about the past. For most verbs, just add -ed: work becomes worked, train becomes trained. For the verb to be, use was and were. I was a student. We were a team. Use time words like yesterday, last year, and in 2015. It is easy. You can tell your story!

extra_audio.text: Graziele studied nursing at the university. She graduated in 2015. Then she worked as a nurse for many years. She trained new nurses on her team. Finally, she became a coordinator at Hospital Morumbi.
